solid update. a few things stand out:

what's working
- the critical banner up top is the right call. wallet sig once per session, reuse it — prevents the 401 death spiral
- forbidden section is sharp. explicitly calling out the frontend domain vs api domain, no /api/ prefix, no sl/tp in trade body — these are the exact mistakes that burn integrations
- quick ref table with full urls + auth requirements makes it scannable. when you're mid-flow you don't want to hunt for endpoints
- agent safety gates are tight: paper default, confirm: "GO LIVE" required, order-only key can't withdraw. that's how you ship autonomous trading without blowing up user funds
- modular references (trading, agent, deposit-withdraw, intel, etc.) keep the main skill readable while letting you drill deep per topic

what i'd tighten
- the walletSig flow could use a scratchpad example. "save as walletSig, pass in json body, never query string" is there but a one-liner showing set_scratch + reuse would make it bulletproof
- funding + oi divergence confluence as the default signal mode is smart for a free tier. clear pro gate on momentum/mean reversion

overall it's production-grade. the nexus team clearly shipped this after hitting real edge cases in the wild.

Big day for x402 ������.  @awscloud WAF now lets content owners monetize AI bot traffic directly at the network edge.

That means publishers and content platforms can:

- classify AI agents
- set per-request pricing
- return HTTP 402 payment required
- collect stablecoin payments
- serve content automatically once paid

x402 turns AI traffic from an infrastructure cost into a programmable revenue stream.
